theoremName: abstract bounded form quotient descent theorem
statement: A trace-compatible quadratic form that is bounded in the transported quotient norm descends uniquely to a bounded closed quadratic form on the quotient Hilbert space.

abstractQuotientDescentPrincipleStatus.label: abstract quotient descent principle
abstractQuotientDescentPrincipleStatus.reason: Trace compatibility makes the value independent of the chosen representative.  Quotient-norm boundedness makes the descended form continuous on the quotient Hilbert space, hence closed.

noModelSpecificInputStatus.label: no model-specific input
noModelSpecificInputStatus.reason: The lemma only uses the quotient Hilbert structure and boundedness.  Finite repair compatibility and bounds are supplied by separate augmented theorems.

proof: If Rf=Rf', then f-f' lies in the null fiber; trace compatibility gives the same form value.
Therefore the form is well-defined on equivalence classes in Ran(R).
The quotient-norm bound gives a continuous bilinear/quadratic form on Ran(R).
Continuous extension to the Hilbert closure X is unique and closed.
